---
title: "The Preventable Chargeback: A Prevention Roadmap"
subtitle: "Chargeback Prediction Model — Cinderhaven Data Platform"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
  pdf:
    toc: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import json
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
import yaml

ROOT = Path("..") if Path("../config.yml").exists() else Path(".")
CONFIG_PATH = ROOT / "config.yml"
FRAMES = ROOT / "output" / "frames"

config = yaml.safe_load(CONFIG_PATH.read_text()) if CONFIG_PATH.exists() else {}
roadmap = pd.read_csv(FRAMES / "prevention_roadmap.csv")
by_archetype = pd.read_csv(FRAMES / "historical_chargebacks_by_archetype.csv")
perf = pd.read_csv(FRAMES / "model_performance.csv")

# Exclude legitimate (non-preventable) from roadmap charts
preventable = roadmap[roadmap["preventability_fraction"] > 0].copy()
preventable = preventable.sort_values("prevention_value", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

total_chargeback = roadmap["historical_loss"].sum()
total_preventable = roadmap["prevention_value"].sum()
preventable_pct = total_preventable / total_chargeback if total_chargeback else 0
model_auc = float(perf["auc"].iloc[0])

ARCHETYPE_DISPLAY = {
    "logistics_overage": "Logistics Overage",
    "data_compliance_error": "Data Compliance Error",
    "asn_timing_infraction": "ASN Timing Infraction",
    "pricing_discrepancy": "Pricing Discrepancy",
    "item_setup_gap": "Item Setup Gap",
    "legitimate": "Legitimate",
}

# Lailara HK teal palette — darkest to lightest for largest to smallest
HK_PALETTE = ["#063d32", "#0a5c4b", "#0e6e5a", "#158f75", "#1fa282"]
CANVAS = "#f5f3ee"
GRIDLINE = "#d9d9d9"
TEXT = "#333333"
```

<div class="section-flag">Prevention Roadmap</div>

## $`{python} f"{total_chargeback:,.0f}"` in annual chargebacks. `{python} f"{preventable_pct:.0%}"` is preventable.

Cinderhaven carries **$`{python} f"{total_chargeback:,.0f}"`** in annual retailer chargebacks. A predictive model trained on three years of Cinderhaven shipment and deduction history identifies **$`{python} f"{total_preventable:,.0f}"`** — `{python} f"{preventable_pct:.0%}"` of the total — as traceable to four fixable upstream data conditions. These are not disputed chargebacks. They are predictable ones.

The model scores each upcoming purchase order before it ships. Every risk score names the specific data condition driving it. This report presents the root-cause breakdown, the ranked prevention roadmap, and the economics of fixing versus disputing.

*This analysis uses Cinderhaven synthetic demonstration data to illustrate a repeatable methodology. The numbers, proportions, and root-cause distribution reflect a realistic CPG brand profile.*

---

## Root-cause breakdown

```{python}
#| label: fig-by-archetype
#| fig-cap: "Historical chargeback amount by root cause. Logistics overage — primarily missing case dimensions and case weight — accounts for the single largest preventable category."

sorted_arch = (
    by_archetype
    .assign(display=lambda df: df["root_cause_archetype"].map(ARCHETYPE_DISPLAY))
    .sort_values("total_amount", ascending=True)
)

n = len(sorted_arch)
colors = (HK_PALETTE * (n // len(HK_PALETTE) + 1))[:n]

fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
    x=sorted_arch["total_amount"],
    y=sorted_arch["display"],
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=colors,
    text=[f"${v:,.0f}" for v in sorted_arch["total_amount"]],
    textposition="outside",
    textfont=dict(family="Source Sans 3, Arial, sans-serif", size=12, color=TEXT),
))

fig.update_layout(
    paper_bgcolor=CANVAS,
    plot_bgcolor=CANVAS,
    font=dict(family="Source Sans 3, Arial, sans-serif", color=TEXT, size=13),
    xaxis=dict(
        showgrid=True, gridcolor=GRIDLINE, gridwidth=1,
        zeroline=False, showticklabels=False, title="",
    ),
    yaxis=dict(showgrid=False, title=""),
    margin=dict(l=0, r=80, t=20, b=20),
    height=320,
    showlegend=False,
)

fig.show()
```

---

## Margin math

The table below translates each root cause into a dollar figure: what it cost historically, what fraction is preventable with targeted data fixes, and the estimated annual recovery.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-margin-math

def fmt_dollar(n):
    if n >= 1_000_000:
        return f"${n/1_000_000:.2f}M"
    if n >= 1_000:
        return f"${n/1_000:.0f}K"
    return f"${n:.0f}"

def fmt_pct(n):
    return f"{n:.0%}"

display_rows = []
for _, row in roadmap.sort_values("prevention_value", ascending=False).iterrows():
    display_rows.append({
        "Root cause": ARCHETYPE_DISPLAY.get(row["root_cause_archetype"], row["root_cause_archetype"]),
        "Historical loss": fmt_dollar(row["historical_loss"]),
        "Preventable": fmt_pct(row["preventability_fraction"]),
        "Prevention value": fmt_dollar(row["prevention_value"]),
        "Chargeback count": int(
            by_archetype.loc[
                by_archetype["root_cause_archetype"] == row["root_cause_archetype"], "count"
            ].values[0]
            if row["root_cause_archetype"] in by_archetype["root_cause_archetype"].values
            else 0
        ),
    })

tbl = pd.DataFrame(display_rows)
tbl.style.hide(axis="index")
```

---

## Ranked prevention roadmap

```{python}
#| label: fig-prevention-value
#| fig-cap: "Prevention value by root cause — estimated annual dollar recovery from targeted data fixes. Logistics overage tops the list at over $240K, driven primarily by case dimension and case weight gaps in the product master."

colors_prev = HK_PALETTE[:len(preventable)]

fig2 = go.Figure(go.Bar(
    x=preventable["prevention_value"],
    y=preventable["root_cause_archetype"].map(ARCHETYPE_DISPLAY),
    orientation="h",
    marker_color=colors_prev,
    text=[f"${v:,.0f}" for v in preventable["prevention_value"]],
    textposition="outside",
    textfont=dict(family="Source Sans 3, Arial, sans-serif", size=12, color=TEXT),
))

fig2.update_layout(
    paper_bgcolor=CANVAS,
    plot_bgcolor=CANVAS,
    font=dict(family="Source Sans 3, Arial, sans-serif", color=TEXT, size=13),
    xaxis=dict(
        showgrid=True, gridcolor=GRIDLINE, gridwidth=1,
        zeroline=False, showticklabels=False, title="",
    ),
    yaxis=dict(showgrid=False, title=""),
    margin=dict(l=0, r=100, t=20, b=20),
    height=260,
    showlegend=False,
)

fig2.show()
```

The actions required to capture this prevention value are data management tasks, not operational overhauls. The roadmap below names the specific fix for each root cause.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-roadmap

roadmap_display = (
    roadmap[roadmap["preventability_fraction"] > 0]
    .sort_values("prevention_value", ascending=False)
    .assign(
        Rank=range(1, len(roadmap[roadmap["preventability_fraction"] > 0]) + 1),
        **{"Root cause": lambda df: df["root_cause_archetype"].map(ARCHETYPE_DISPLAY)},
        **{"Annual prevention value": lambda df: df["prevention_value"].apply(fmt_dollar)},
        **{"Required action": lambda df: df["fix_description"]},
    )[["Rank", "Root cause", "Annual prevention value", "Required action"]]
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
roadmap_display.style.hide(axis="index")
```

---

## Recovery versus prevention

The deductions team currently disputes chargebacks after they post. The industry win rate on retailer disputes is 30–45%. At a 40% win rate, recovering $`{python} f"{total_chargeback:,.0f}"` in annual chargebacks yields roughly **$`{python} f"{total_chargeback * 0.40:,.0f}"`** — before dispute labor costs.

Prevention requires no dispute process. Fixing the data conditions identified above eliminates the chargeback at source. The model's AUC of **`{python} f"{model_auc:.4f}"`** means that `{python} f"{model_auc:.0%}"` of actual chargebacks are flagged before shipment, giving the brand a pre-ship intervention window.

The math is straightforward: **$`{python} f"{total_preventable:,.0f}"`** in preventable chargebacks recovered at 100% is worth more than $`{python} f"{total_chargeback:,.0f}"` recovered at 40%, and it eliminates the working capital drag of post-ship disputes.

<div class="disclaimer">
This analysis was produced using Cinderhaven synthetic demonstration data. All dollar figures, chargeback counts, and root-cause distributions are illustrative of a real methodology, not actual Cinderhaven financials.
</div>
